Books.title: The Book of Why
Books.author: Judea Pearl
Books.description: A clear primer on causal thinking: why it is different from correlation, and why it matters for science that aims to intervene. It also gives perspective on how recent the formal mathematical language of causation is — and why that history still shapes how we argue about evidence.

Books.title: The Emperor of All Maladies
Books.author: Siddhartha Mukherjee
Books.description: A historical biography of cancer that makes modern oncology feel less like a snapshot and more like an evolving story. It helps explain why certain clinical conventions exist, and how older assumptions still echo in present-day treatment decisions.

Books.title: Malignant
Books.author: Vinay Prasad
Books.description: A guide to reading medical evidence with a skeptical, patient-centered lens — especially in oncology. It highlights how incentives can diverge from patient benefit, and offers practical instincts for spotting over-interpretation in trials and headlines.

Books.title: Everything Is Tuberculosis
Books.author: John Green
Books.description: A global-health perspective on tuberculosis that pulls you out of a Western-centric frame. It’s a reminder of how common and deadly TB still is, and how social structure and access to care shape what we call “progress” in medicine.

Books.title: The Illustrated Guide to Machine Learning
Books.author: Josh Starmer
Books.description: Exceptionally clear visual explanations of core machine learning concepts, with a strong emphasis on intuition over jargon. Great for building a mental model you can actually use when choosing methods and debugging results.

Books.title: The Illustrated Guide to Deep Learning & Neural Networks
Books.author: Josh Starmer
Books.description: A friendly, visual introduction to neural networks and deep learning that removes unnecessary mystique. It’s especially useful for translating buzzwords into concrete mental pictures and implementation-level understanding.

Books.title: The Song of the Cell
Books.author: Siddhartha Mukherjee
Books.description: A narrative exploration of the cell as the fundamental unit of biology, and how cell-based thinking reshaped modern medicine. It connects the history of ideas to the practical reality of therapies, diagnostics, and the limits of reductionism.

Books.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
Books.author: Daniel Kahneman
Books.description: A deep dive into dual-system thinking, cognitive biases, and how humans evaluate evidence under uncertainty. It’s highly relevant to scientific reasoning — especially when interpreting noisy data, p-values, and compelling stories.

Podcasts.title: ISAPP Podcast
Podcasts.creator: International Scientific Association for Probiotics and Prebiotics (ISAPP)
Podcasts.description: Short primers from leaders in microbiome and probiotic research, explained directly by the experts. A good way to stay anchored in mechanisms, definitions, and what the evidence actually supports.

Podcasts.title: Night Science
Podcasts.creator: Itai Yanai & Martin Lercher
Podcasts.description: Focuses on the creative, non-linear side of science — how ideas actually form before they become polished papers. It’s an honest look at exploration, taste, and the messy path from curiosity to a research question.

Podcasts.title: Sensible Medicine
Podcasts.creator: Sensible Medicine Authors and Editors
Podcasts.description: Examines how we know whether medical interventions truly help patients, with a critical lens on industry-sponsored clinical trials. The discussions are practical and evidence-first, with attention to real-world decision-making.

Podcasts.title: Inflammatory Content
Podcasts.creator: Kellen Cavagnero, Ph.D.
Podcasts.description: Dr. Kellen Cavagnero walks through complex topics in inflammation together with domain experts in a clear, digestible way. Great for learning immunology by following concrete papers and specific biological questions.

Podcasts.title: Plenary Session (Vinay Prasad)
Podcasts.creator: Vinay Prasad, MD MPH
Podcasts.description: Deep dives into medical evidence, oncology trials, and how to critically read the literature. The podcast is currently paused, but the back catalogue is still a useful reference for evidence hygiene.

Podcasts.title: The Nextflow Podcast
Podcasts.creator: Seqera
Podcasts.description: Conversations around reproducible workflows, data pipelines, and best practices in modern computational biology. Especially useful if you care about operationalizing analysis: versioning, containers, and portability.

Podcasts.title: The Test Set (Posit)
Podcasts.creator: Posit, PBC
Podcasts.description: Discussions on data science, statistics, reproducibility, and tooling with a strong applied perspective. It’s a good bridge between method details and how teams actually ship analysis.

Podcasts.title: Asimov Press
Podcasts.creator: Asimov Press
Podcasts.description: Long-form conversations and essays on science, technology, and policy aimed at thoughtful scientific audiences. A nice complement to paper-reading when you want broader context and synthesis.

YouTube.title: Bioinformatics Dot Ca
YouTube.creator: Bioinformatics.ca
YouTube.description: Comprehensive lecture-style series covering metagenomics, RNA-seq, R, pathway analysis, and more. I come back to it when I want a structured, training-oriented walkthrough rather than a quick tutorial.

YouTube.title: Australia Biocommons
YouTube.creator: Australian BioCommons
YouTube.description: Training resources and talks on bioinformatics infrastructure, reproducibility, and best practices. Particularly valuable for the operational side of bioinformatics: tooling, platforms, and community standards.

YouTube.title: 3Blue1Brown
YouTube.creator: 3Blue1Brown
YouTube.description: Intuitive math explanations with exceptional visual clarity, great for building deep intuition. Useful when you want to actually understand linear algebra, probability, and dynamical ideas behind models.

YouTube.title: StatQuest with Josh Starmer
YouTube.creator: Josh Starmer
YouTube.description: One of the clearest places to build intuition for statistics, machine learning, and RNA-seq analysis without getting lost in jargon. Especially useful when you want to understand what a method is doing before you worry about its implementation details.

YouTube.title: Serrano Academy
YouTube.creator: Serrano.Academy
YouTube.description: Clear, practical machine learning and deep learning lessons that emphasize intuition and implementation. Good for bridging the gap between theory and code without drowning in formalism.

YouTube.title: The Net Ninja
YouTube.creator: Net Ninja
YouTube.description: Friendly tutorials on modern web development and programming fundamentals. Great for picking up a framework quickly and building the muscle memory to ship small, clean projects.

YouTube.title: Fireship
YouTube.creator: Fireship
YouTube.description: Fast, high-signal overviews of modern developer tools and frameworks; great for quick orientation. I use it to get the lay of the land before diving into docs.

YouTube.title: Liquidbrain Bioinformatics
YouTube.creator: LiquidBrain Bioinformatics
YouTube.description: Applied bioinformatics tutorials and walkthroughs, often focused on practical workflows. Helpful when you want to see a tool used end-to-end and understand the typical failure modes.

YouTube.title: Deepia
YouTube.creator: Deepia
YouTube.description: Educational content focused on deep learning concepts and learning resources. A useful supplement when you want a different angle on core ideas and how to study them effectively.

YouTube.title: Alexander Amini
YouTube.creator: Alexander Amini
YouTube.description: MIT-level introductions to AI and deep learning, blending theory, intuition, and practical examples. Great when you want a course-like arc that still stays grounded in implementation details.

YouTube.title: Meren Lab
YouTube.creator: Meren Lab
YouTube.description: Open-source microbiome research content, PhD defenses, and excellent Anvi’o tutorials. Ideal for learning by watching real analyses and hearing the reasoning behind decisions.

YouTube.title: DigitalSreeni
YouTube.creator: DigitalSreeni
YouTube.description: Bioinformatics workflows, tool walkthroughs, and practical how-to content. Useful for quickly sanity-checking a pipeline step or seeing a common tool used in context.
